data.title: Bitcoin Mining Profitability Hits 14-Month Low Amid Price Drop
data.description:
Bitcoin's profitability for miners has reached its lowest point in 14 months, according to data from CryptoQuant. According to ChainCatcher, the miner profitability index has fallen to 21, marking the lowest level since November 2024.

CryptoQuant attributes this decline to a significant drop in Bitcoin prices this week, coupled with the current mining difficulty, resulting in extremely low miner revenues. Despite a consecutive five-cycle decrease in the Bitcoin network's hash rate, which is now at its lowest since September 2025, the situation remains challenging for miners.

In addition to the low revenue indicated by these metrics, some miners have been severely affected by a recent severe winter storm that swept across the eastern United States, causing ice and snow damage in multiple states.

data.title: Coinbase launches Infinex (INX) spot trading
data.description: According to official sources, Coinbase has announced the launch of spot trading for Infinex (INX). If liquidity conditions are met, the INX-USD trading pair will begin trading later today in supported regions.

data.title: MOLT Market Cap Experiences Significant Surge
data.description: MOLT's market capitalization has reached a peak of $124 million, currently standing at $81.49 million, marking a 24-hour increase of 2267.4%. According to Foresight News, this substantial growth highlights the volatility and rapid changes in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Tether's net profit is projected to exceed $10 billion by 2025, with gold reserves exceeding $17.4 billion.
data.description: Tether reported net profits exceeding $10 billion in 2025, primarily driven by growth in its USDT stablecoin and increased investments in U.S. Treasury bonds. The company held $6.3 billion in excess reserves at the end of the year to support its $186.5 billion USDT liabilities, making it one of the largest holders of U.S. government debt, with exposure reaching $141 billion. Its reserves also included $17.4 billion worth of gold and $8.4 billion worth of Bitcoin. (CoinDesk)

data.title: The US Senate passed a spending bill that would fund most departments of the federal government.
data.description: On March 30th local time, the U.S. Senate passed a spending bill to fund most departments of the federal government and sent it to the House of Representatives for consideration. Since House members are not in Washington and will not return until Monday (February 2nd), a Senate vote will inevitably result in a partial government shutdown beginning in the early hours of March 31st. House Speaker Mike Johnson stated in a private call with House Republicans that the most likely way for the House to pass the bill is through a fast-track vote on Monday night. (CCTV)

data.title: The White House Office of Management and Budget confirmed that some government departments will be shut down today.
data.description: The White House Office of Management and Budget issued a memo confirming that some government departments will shut down today. The memo stated that, given the current situation clearly indicates that Congress will be unable to complete its work before the funding deadline (11:59 PM ET on January 30), affected agencies should now begin implementing plans for an orderly shutdown.

data.title: U.S. Government Agencies Face Shutdown Amid Budget Deadline
data.description: The White House Office of Management and Budget has issued a memorandum confirming that certain government departments will shut down today. According to Odaily, the email indicates that given the current situation, it is clear Congress will not complete its work before the funding deadline expires at 11:59 PM Eastern Time on January 30. Affected agencies are now advised to begin implementing orderly shutdown procedures.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Poised for Growth by 2026, Says Bitwise CIO
data.description:
Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan has indicated that the cryptocurrency market is nearing the end of a bear market phase, setting the stage for more robust development by 2026. According to ChainCatcher, Hougan shared in an interview that while cryptocurrencies have been in a bear market for much of 2025, with many altcoins dropping over 60%, Bitcoin has managed to avoid larger losses due to continued buying by corporations and ETFs.

Hougan described the current situation as a 'narrowing bottom,' characterized by weak ETF fund flows and low retail participation. He anticipates that Bitcoin will trade sideways between $75,000 and $100,000 in the first half of this year. Hougan reiterated his belief that Bitcoin's price could reach approximately $6.5 million per coin over the next 20 years. He emphasized that this projection is based not on accelerated adoption but on the continued growth of global debt, currency issuance, and currency devaluation.

Hougan views Bitcoin as an upgraded version of gold, noting that central banks are only beginning to understand its role. He stated, "As long as the future does not differ significantly from the past 15 years, we can achieve our goals. It's just a matter of time."

data.description:
JPMorgan analysts say Bitcoin futures are likely oversold, while gold and silver futures are overbought.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/jpmorgan-sees-bitcoin-futures-oversold/

data.title: DAO Project to Reinvest Dormant Ethereum Assets for Network Security
data.description: The DAO project, which was pivotal in the 2016 hack that resulted in Ethereum's hard fork, is making a comeback after ten years. According to NS3.AI, the initiative intends to deploy more than 75,000 ETH from dormant investor assets to enhance Ethereum's network security through staking and yield generation. The reinvested returns are designed to fortify the Ethereum ecosystem and bolster its position in the global financial infrastructure.

data.description:
The European Commission has warned 12 EU member states for failing to implement crypto tax reporting rules fully.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/eu-issues-crypto-tax-warnings-to-12-states/

data.title: Negotiations Between OpenAI and Nvidia for $100 Billion Deal Hit a Snag
data.description: The Kobeissi Letter posted on X. Talks concerning a potential $100 billion agreement between OpenAI and Nvidia have come to a halt. Reports indicate that Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, has privately expressed criticism regarding OpenAI's business strategy. The discussions, which were anticipated to significantly impact the tech industry, are now uncertain as both parties reassess their positions. The outcome of these negotiations remains to be seen, with industry observers closely monitoring any developments.

data.title: Crypto Market Experiences Consolidation Amid Pressure
data.description: Crypto KOL Ted posted on X that the cryptocurrency market is currently experiencing a phase of consolidation under pressure. This observation comes as market participants closely monitor the fluctuations and trends within the digital asset space. The consolidation phase indicates a period where prices stabilize after significant movements, often leading to a potential breakout or further decline. Market analysts suggest that this phase could be crucial for determining the future direction of various cryptocurrencies. Investors are advised to stay informed and cautious during this period of market uncertainty.

data.title: Gold suffered its biggest single-day drop in 40 years, while silver plunged more than 36%, setting a new record.
data.description: On January 30th, gold experienced its biggest drop in 40 years, while silver saw its largest intraday decline in history. Spot gold prices fell by more than 12% at one point, hitting a low of $4,682 per ounce, the largest single-day drop since the early 1980s. By the close, spot gold was down 9.25% at $4,880 per ounce. Spot silver plummeted by more than 36% at one point, marking its largest intraday drop in history, hitting a low of $74.28 per ounce. By the close, spot silver was down 26.42% at $85.259 per ounce.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Milan Uncertain About Future Role
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan has expressed uncertainty about his future role following the end of his current interim term this weekend. According to Odaily, Milan stated that he will continue in his current position until a successor is confirmed. Whether he will attend the Federal Reserve meeting in March depends on the Senate's timeline for confirming Walsh's nomination. If the confirmation process takes more than six weeks, Milan will participate in the meeting.

data.title: U.S. Senate Approves Funding Bill to Prevent Government Shutdown
data.description: The U.S. Senate has passed a spending bill to fund most federal government departments, moving it to the House of Representatives for consideration. According to PANews, the funding for several federal departments was set to run out on January 30. Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer's spokesperson announced on January 29 that an agreement had been reached between Senate Democrats and Republicans to prevent a partial government shutdown. The agreement includes advancing the government funding bill to provide resources for most departments for the remainder of the fiscal year, while granting a two-week temporary funding extension for the Department of Homeland Security. This extension aims to allow further negotiations on limiting the department's immigration enforcement actions.

data.title: Quantum Computing and Cryptographic Security: A Reality Check
data.description: Crypto KOL Eli5defi on X highlights the growing concern over quantum computing's potential impact on cryptographic security. Recent headlines have sparked fears that quantum computers could soon break current cryptographic systems. However, experts suggest that while quantum computing is advancing, it is not yet at a stage where it poses an immediate threat to cryptographic security. The discussion emphasizes the need for ongoing research and development in quantum-resistant cryptographic methods to ensure future security. Eli5defi underscores the importance of staying informed about technological advancements and their implications for the crypto industry.
